name: IEEE Conference/Journal
notes: IEEE two-column format. Use \cite{} (numeric). 8-page limit for conferences (+ refs). Double-blind review. No author info in submission.
writing_guide:
TONE: Formal and technical. Prefer passive voice for methods ('The system was evaluated...') but active voice is acceptable for contributions ('We propose...'). Be concise; every sentence must earn its space in 8 pages.

ABSTRACT: Exactly 150-200 words. Structure: context (1-2 sentences), problem (1 sentence), approach (2-3 sentences), results with specific numbers (2-3 sentences), significance (1 sentence). No citations, no acronyms undefined.

STRUCTURE: Dense two-column format demands tight writing. 'Proposed Method' section is expected (not just 'Methods'). Use numbered equations heavily and reference them ('as shown in (3)'). Include algorithm pseudocode for any non-trivial procedure. Put 'Complexity Analysis' subsection if relevant.

CITATIONS: Use numeric \cite{}. 30-50 total references typical. 8-15 in Introduction, 15-25 in Related Work. Cite IEEE transactions and conference papers preferentially.

FIGURES/TABLES: 5-8 display items. System architecture diagram expected. Comparison tables with bold best results. Figures must be readable at column width (3.5in).

REVIEWER EXPECTATIONS: Strong quantitative evaluation, comparison with SOTA on standard benchmarks, ablation study, clear novelty statement.

DO NOT: Use self-citations that reveal identity (double-blind). Do not exceed 8 pages for main content. Do not use color as the only distinguishing feature in plots. Do not omit standard deviation or confidence intervals.
